meta.title: cide

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Root, words and meanings transcribed faithfully from the source PDF; a small number of well-attested real words on the same root are included where the source list was too short to form a complete family (4 to 8 members). Parts, glosses, context sentences, mentor note, attempt and construct options are the teaching layer, authored for the Language Garden (LANGUAGE_GARDEN_BIBLE §10).

meta.reviewer_notes: Batch 2: transcribed from the owner's roots (Greek and Latin) reference; 3 taught, 2 reach.

root.label: cide
root.mentor_note: cide names the killing of something specific; the rest of the word says exactly what.

attempt.direction_prompt: A crime against property, or the killing of a person?

context_sentences: Detectives treated the case as a homicide from the moment they saw the scene.
The city’s homicide rate had fallen for six straight years by the time the report was published.
The charity’s helpline exists for exactly the moment someone is considering suicide.
Rates of suicide in the region had dropped noticeably since the new crisis line opened.
The farm switched to a milder insecticide after bee populations nearby began to decline.
One heavy dose of insecticide wiped out both the pests and every ladybird in the greenhouse.
The tribunal spent a decade prosecuting those responsible for the genocide.
Survivors of the genocide have spent thirty years making sure the world does not forget it.

construct_options.text: The deliberate killing of a large national or ethnic group
The accidental killing of one person by another
A substance used to kill a variety of pests

context_sentences: The ancient tragedy turns on a single act of patricide the hero commits without recognising his victim.
Historians still argue over whether the emperor’s death was patricide or a simple accident.

construct_options.text: The killing of one’s own father
The killing of one’s own child
A disagreement between a father and his child
